questionHtml:
At 700 ºC, the equilibrium constant, Kc, for the reaction is 1.075 × 108.

2H2 (g) + S2 (g) ⇌ 2H2S (g)

Which relationship is always correct for the equilibrium at this temperature?

A. [H2S]2 < [H2]2 [S2]

B. [S2] = 2[H2S]

C. [H2S] < [S2]

D. [H2S]2 > [H2]2[S2]

meta.title: At 700 ºC, the equilibrium constant, Kc, for the reaction is 1.075 × 108. 2H2 (g) + S2 (g) ⇌ 2H2S (g) Which relationship is always correct for the equilibrium at this temperature?
